mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) captures consumer spending momentum that drives corporate revenue expectations and S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive RSAFS changes are not followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in > 50% of months over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, labor market slack triggers downward revisions to earnings forecasts and risk-off positioning, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE > 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates combined with declining industrial production create a restrictive financial and real economy conjuncture that compresses profit margins and equity multiples, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 2.0% and INDPRO below its 12-month trailing maximum
failure_condition: The joint signal does not produce negative 20-day returns in at least 55% of triggered periods over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary easing cycles, month-over-month declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate applied to future cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS is declining, SPX 5-day returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in industrial production indicate rising manufacturing output and capacity utilization that support earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive 3-month changes in INDPRO coincide with negative 60-day SPX returns in more than 45% of quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth signals aggregate demand strength that drives top-line revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth prints are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index inflation above 4% year-over-year pressures real incomes and forces tighter Fed policy, which expands equity risk premiums and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of months when CPIAUCSL YoY > 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 1 million units signal a contraction in residential investment and downstream demand for durable goods, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: HOUST < 1000 is followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of triggered instances.
